race.name: Gran Fondo Hincapie
race.tagline: George Hincapie's Blue Ridge playground. 83 miles. 8,000 feet. Three timed climbs. Skyuka Mountain at 8.7%. The Southeast's premier gran fondo, born from 17 Tours de France.

vitals.registration: Online via BikeReg. Three tiers: Early Bird (jersey, bib shorts, socks, arm warmers, Road ID), Standard (jersey, Road ID), Late/On-site (jersey mailed post-event). Cost: ~$175-275 depending on tier and distance.
vitals.feed_zones: 4 fully stocked rest stops on the Gran route; fewer on shorter distances. On-course SAG support, free participant photography.
vitals.cutoff_time: Course support has time limits. Riders must maintain reasonable pace.
route_options: Gran: 83 miles, ~8,100 ft climbing — 3 timed climbs including Skyuka Mountain and Green River Cove
Grandito: 67 miles — the Gran's climbs with the Medio's distance, 2 timed climbs
Medio: 50 miles — rolling Blue Ridge foothills through North and South Carolina, 2 rest stops
Piccolo: 15 miles, ~1,245 ft — family-friendly loop from Hotel Domestique

climate.primary: Southeast US autumn — warm days, cool mornings
climate.description: Mid-October in the South Carolina Upstate delivers the region's best cycling weather, though the Southeast's reputation for unpredictability is well-earned. Mornings start cool in the mid-50s at the Hotel Domestique start, but Blue Ridge afternoon sun can push temps into the mid-70s on exposed climbs. The 2025 edition saw 'sunny and warm' conditions that forced many riders to take on extra hydration and salts. October humidity is lower than summer but still present. The elevation changes between the valley floor (~1,100 ft at Travelers Rest) and the mountain climbs (~3,000 ft on Skyuka) create meaningful temperature swings within the same ride.
challenges: Southeast humidity persists into October, amplifying perceived heat on sustained climbs
Temperature swings between valley (~1,100 ft) and summit (~3,000 ft) — 10-15 degree difference possible
Afternoon heat on exposed road climbs in the mid-70s can compound fatigue late in the Gran
Occasional autumn rain systems can make technical descents like Skyuka dangerous

terrain.primary: Paved Blue Ridge foothill roads with sustained mountain climbs
terrain.surface: All paved roads through the Blue Ridge foothills of upstate South Carolina and neighboring Polk County, North Carolina. Well-maintained mountain roads with some rough patches on secondary routes. The route traces George Hincapie's personal Tour de France training grounds — roads he rode thousands of times preparing for July in France.
features: Skyuka Mountain — 4 miles at 8.7% average gradient, 1,814 ft gain, the signature timed climb
Green River Cove — thrilling switchbacks through Polk County, NC, a second timed segment
Final climb to Hotel Domestique finish — the third timed segment to close the Gran
Lake Road twisties — rolling technical turns that demand attention early in the ride
Carbon clincher rim brake wheels prohibited on Gran route due to Skyuka descent steepness

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 67. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67.

course_description.character: George Hincapie trained on these roads for 17 Tours de France, and the Gran route makes you understand why. The 83-mile course from Hotel Domestique punches north into the Blue Ridge foothills, crossing into North Carolina's Polk County for the signature climbs before looping back to the Travelers Rest finish. Three timed segments — Skyuka Mountain, Green River Cove, and the final climb to the finish — structure the suffering around sustained, gradient-heavy climbing. Between the major climbs, rolling Blue Ridge countryside and Lake Road's technical turns keep the legs honest. The autumn foliage through dense forest canopy creates postcard scenery that's easy to miss when you're redlined on Skyuka's 8.7% gradient. This is a road event on paved surfaces throughout, but the mountainous character, the altitude variation from 1,100 ft to nearly 3,000 ft, and the relentless vertical accumulation make it one of the hardest gran fondos in the Eastern United States.
course_description.signature_challenge: Skyuka Mountain. Four miles at 8.7% average gradient, 1,814 feet of vertical gain, a summit at 2,967 feet, and a technical descent so steep that carbon clincher rim brakes are banned from the course. This is where the Gran Fondo Hincapie earns the right to carry a Tour de France veteran's name.

logistics.airport: Greenville-Spartanburg International (GSP) — 30 minutes to Travelers Rest. Strong domestic connections, reasonable fares. Charlotte Douglas International (CLT) is 90 minutes and offers more flight options.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotel Domestique (10 Road of Vines, Travelers Rest) is race HQ — luxury boutique hotel founded by George Hincapie in the Blue Ridge foothills. Premium pricing but unbeatable proximity. Downtown Greenville (10 miles south) offers a full range of hotels at various price points. Embassy Suites downtown connects directly to Travelers Rest via the Swamp Rabbit Trail. Book 2-3 months ahead — the event weekend fills local lodging. Airbnb options in Travelers Rest and northern Greenville County provide alternatives.
logistics.food: Greenville's food scene punches well above its weight for a mid-sized Southern city. Phil Gaimon highlighted Tandem Crepery ('Get there early — long line'), Methodical Coffee, and the Commons along the Swamp Rabbit Trail. Downtown Greenville's Main Street offers extensive dining. The event weekend includes a Michelin-starred celebrity chef dinner (Chef Matthew Accarrino) and a post-ride festival with complimentary food and beverages for riders and families.

history.origin_story: Gran Fondo Hincapie was born in 2012, the year George Hincapie retired from professional cycling after one of the most decorated careers in American road cycling history. George had started 17 Tours de France — more than any American ever — and was the only rider to support Lance Armstrong through all seven of his Tour de France victories as a member of US Postal and Discovery Channel. He also domestiqued for Tour winners Alberto Contador (2007) and Cadel Evans (2011). George won a Tour de France stage in 2005, claimed Gent-Wevelgem in 2001, and earned three US Professional Road Race titles. When he retired, he and brother Rich created the Gran Fondo as a way to share the Blue Ridge roads where George had trained for France — routes radiating from Hotel Domestique, the luxury boutique hotel the Hincapie family built in Travelers Rest. What started as a single Greenville event has grown into a five-event national series spanning Greenville, Chattanooga, Lehigh Valley, Bentonville, and Merced.
notable_moments: 2012: Inaugural edition from Hotel Domestique — George Hincapie's retirement celebration ride
2015: Event establishes itself as the Southeast's premier gran fondo with growing national draw
2020: Held during the pandemic as one of the first cycling events permitted — modified COVID-safe format

2024: Hurricane Helene devastates Polk County three weeks before the event; organizers pivot from cancellation to a modified 19-mile loop format, raising $750,000+ for relief. Cavendish, Wiggins, and Ullrich ride to support the cause
2025: 14th edition draws 2,000+ riders and earns 'Best Charity Fondo in North America' recognition
2026: 15th anniversary edition scheduled for October 17 — courses may be subject to change due to lingering Hurricane Helene road damage
history.reputation: The most legitimate celebrity gran fondo in North America. George Hincapie's name carries genuine weight in the cycling world, and the Blue Ridge terrain delivers authentic suffering that justifies the prestige. The Hurricane Helene relief response cemented the event's reputation as more than branded cycling tourism.

biased_opinion.summary: George Hincapie spent 17 years as professional cycling's most trusted domestique — the rider who sacrificed personal glory so others could stand on the Tour de France podium. His retirement project is the opposite philosophy: an event designed entirely for your experience. Gran Fondo Hincapie routes through the Blue Ridge foothill roads where Hincapie actually trained for July in France, summiting Skyuka Mountain at 8.7% and navigating Green River Cove's switchbacks — the same climbs that built the engine behind nine Tour de France champions. The 83-mile Gran with 8,100 feet of climbing is legitimate suffering, not a branded fun ride. But the Hotel Domestique base, the Michelin-starred chef dinner, the post-ride festival, and the parade of cycling legends create an experience layer that most gran fondos can only aspire to. The 2024 Hurricane Helene pivot — when the event raised $750,000 for the devastated Polk County communities that had hosted the fondo for 13 years — showed that this isn't corporate cycling tourism. It's an event with a soul.
strengths: Skyuka Mountain — 4 miles at 8.7%, the Southeast's most punishing timed climb
Cycling royalty guest list: Cavendish, Wiggins, Ullrich, Evans, Hesjedal, Dempsey
15 years of refined operations from Hotel Domestique base
Hurricane Helene relief response demonstrated genuine community bonds
Greenville's cycling infrastructure (Swamp Rabbit Trail, GSP airport access)
weaknesses: Premium pricing ($175-275) with brand premium baked in
Field is recreational tourism, not competitive racing depth
October Southeast heat can punish the unprepared on sustained climbs
Carbon clincher rim brakes banned from Gran route — equipment restriction
Hurricane Helene road damage may affect 2026 course routing
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The most legitimate celebrity gran fondo in North America. Skyuka Mountain earns the suffering. The Hincapie name earns the prestige. The $750K Hurricane Helene relief effort earns the respect.

final_verdict.one_liner: George Hincapie's Tour de France training roads. Skyuka Mountain at 8.7%. The Southeast's premier gran fondo with 15 years of pedigree.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if riding a Tour de France domestique's personal training roads alongside cycling legends appeals to you. Yes if you want a legitimate 83-mile, 8,100-foot climbing challenge wrapped in a luxury cycling weekend. Skip if you need competitive racing depth — this is participatory, not a race. Skip if premium pricing for a branded experience isn't your thing.
final_verdict.alternatives: For more climbing challenge in the Southeast: Assault on Mt. Mitchell (102 mi, 10,357 ft). For a similar celebrity gran fondo: Sagan Fondo. For the Hincapie experience elsewhere: Gran Fondo Hincapie Chattanooga, Lehigh Valley, or Bentonville editions.

videos.title: Hincapie Gran Fondo: Greenville, SC 2020
videos.description: Hincapie Gran Fondo - Greenville, SC 2020. Jumped into this Gran Fondo because it was the first event that was allowed in 2020, due to the pandemic. I registered for the Gran race distance (80 miles), but skipped the green river cove switchbacks and added the end of the medio course for a little over 70 miles. Filmed on GoPro Hero9.

videos.title: Hincapie Gran Fondo Greenville 2022...plus tips and insight from the ride
videos.description: Stay tuned (or just forward) to the end for some tips and insight on the ride.
videos.curation_reason: Recent race recap with highest view count that promises specific tips and insights from the ride experience.

videos.title: 2023 HINCAPIE GRAN FONDO - GREENVILLE SOUTH CAROLINA
videos.description: Crash - 1:30 --  Turn on Lake Road 3:30 -- Lake Road Twisties 5:00 -- Skyuka Climb 13:15 --  Skyuka Descent - 14:00 -- Post Finish - 19:30 -- Pictures 20:40. I didn't spend a lot of time on this one.  Basically just wanted to get it up.  I need to up my game and get a GoPro Remote so I can make sure that I'm recording.  It was a great day with absolutely perfect weather....It's the start, which is chaotic.....then Lake Road.....climbing Skyuka and then descending Skyuka.

rider_intel.search_text: The Gran Fondo Hincapie takes place in Greenville, South Carolina, utilizing the area's cycling infrastructure including the Swamp Rabbit Trail, a rails-to-trails conversion that connects Greenville to Travelers Rest. The route features Paris Mountain, described as a legendary climb with significant history from hosting National Road Race Championships for four to five years. Riders mention the beautiful downtown area with waterfalls and cafes accessible via bike paths. The Swamp Rabbit Trail allows car-free travel from downtown to accommodations like Embassy Suites. Local cycling amenities include numerous shops and cafes along the path, with popular spots being Tandem Crepery, Methodical Coffee, and the Commons. The area offers excellent cycling infrastructure with dedicated bike paths that avoid car traffic, making it rider-friendly for both the event and general cycling activities.
